# cloudfly_instance (Resource)

Manages a CloudFly compute instance.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "cloudfly_instance" "example" {
  name        = "example-instance"
  region      = "HN-Cloud01"
  flavor_type = "Standard"
  image_name  = "CentOS-7.9"
  ram         = 1
  vcpus       = 1
  disk        = 20
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `disk` (Number)
- `flavor_type` (String)
- `image_name` (String)
- `ram` (Number)
- `region` (String)
- `vcpus` (Number)

### Optional

- `auto_backup` (Boolean)
- `enable_ipv6` (Boolean)
- `enable_private_network` (Boolean)
- `name` (String)
- `ssh_key_ids` (List of Number)

### Read-Only

- `access_ipv4` (String)
- `created` (String)
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `status` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import cloudfly_instance.example "<instance-id>"
```
